section_name: General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution Sold Through Private Vendor
text:
(a) The sponsor of a specialty license plate may contract with the private vendor authorized under Subchapter J for the marketing and sale of the specialty license plate.

(b) The fee for issuance of a specialty license plate described by Subsection (a) is the amount established under Section 504.851 (Contract with Private Vendor) .

(c) Notwithstanding any other law, from each fee received for the issuance of a specialty license plate described by Subsection (a), the department shall:

(1) deduct the administrative costs described by Section 504.601 (General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution) (a)(2);

(2) deposit to the credit of the account designated by the law authorizing the specialty license plate the portion of the fee for the sale of the plate that the state would ordinarily receive under the contract described by Section 504.851 (Contract with Private Vendor) (a); and

(3) pay to the private vendor the remainder of the fee.

(d) A sponsor of a specialty license plate authorized to be issued under this subchapter before November 19, 2009, may reestablish its specialty license plate under Sections 504.601 (General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution) and 504.702 and be credited its previous deposit with the department if a contract entered into by the sponsor under Subsection (a) terminates.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 504. License Plates
##### § 504.6011 — General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution Sold Through Private Vendor

(a) The sponsor of a specialty license plate may contract with the private vendor authorized under Subchapter J for the marketing and sale of the specialty license plate.

(b) The fee for issuance of a specialty license plate described by Subsection (a) is the amount established under Section 504.851 (Contract with Private Vendor) .

(c) Notwithstanding any other law, from each fee received for the issuance of a specialty license plate described by Subsection (a), the department shall:

    (1) deduct the administrative costs described by Section 504.601 (General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution) (a)(2);

    (2) deposit to the credit of the account designated by the law authorizing the specialty license plate the portion of the fee for the sale of the plate that the state would ordinarily receive under the contract described by Section 504.851 (Contract with Private Vendor) (a); and

    (3) pay to the private vendor the remainder of the fee.

(d) A sponsor of a specialty license plate authorized to be issued under this subchapter before November 19, 2009, may reestablish its specialty license plate under Sections 504.601 (General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution) and 504.702 and be credited its previous deposit with the department if a contract entered into by the sponsor under Subsection (a) terminates.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1296 (H.B.2357), Sec. 203, eff. January 1, 2012.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The sponsor of a specialty license plate may contract with the private vendor authorized under Subchapter J for the marketing and sale of the specialty license plate.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The fee for issuance of a specialty license plate described by Subsection (a) is the amount established under Section 504.851 (Contract with Private Vendor) .

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: Notwithstanding any other law, from each fee received for the issuance of a specialty license plate described by Subsection (a), the department shall:
deduct the administrative costs described by Section 504.601 (General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution) (a)(2);

deposit to the credit of the account designated by the law authorizing the specialty license plate the portion of the fee for the sale of the plate that the state would ordinarily receive under the contract described by Section 504.851 (Contract with Private Vendor) (a); and
pay to the private vendor the remainder of the fee.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: A sponsor of a specialty license plate authorized to be issued under this subchapter before November 19, 2009, may reestablish its specialty license plate under Sections 504.601 (General Provisions Applicable to Specialty License Plates for General Distribution) and 504.702 and be credited its previous deposit with the department if a contract entered into by the sponsor under Subsection (a) terminates.
